data.title: United Airlines Stock (UAL) Hits Turbulence on Plan to Cut Travel Rewards
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) stock is down 4% in early trading on Feb. 19 after the carrier announced changes to its travel rewards.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Management at Chicago-based United said that they are revamping their MileagePlus frequent flyer program to reward travelers that use the airline's credit cards. Going forward, United will reward travelers with more miles and lower redemption rates, including for some of its long-haul business-class seats, provided they also use the company's credit cards.

United said the changes are being made to reward its highest-spending customers. The shift marks the biggest shake-up to United Airlines' rewards program in more than a decade, when the carrier began rewarding customers for how much they spent rather than for how far they flew. Investors have been quick to give the latest change a thumbs down, pushing UAL stock lower.

The changes take effect April 2.

United Airlines' Credit Cards

The changes to United's loyalty program are part of a wider trend among airlines to reward frequent flyer program members for how much money they spend. About a decade ago, the major carriers tweaked their loyalty programs to reward customers for dollars spent rather than miles traveled.

United is encouraging customers to sign up for its credit cards by offering additional perks like no fees for checked bags and earlier boarding. Going forward, United MileagePlus credit card holders will get more miles per dollar spent on United flights compared with customers without the airline's card. United will also now allow customers with one of its credit cards to redeem their miles for flights at a 10% discount.

United Airlines partners exclusively with JPMorgan Chase (JPM) to issue its co-branded credit cards in the U.S.

Is UAL Stock a Buy?

United Airlines' stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average UAL price target of $142.32 implies 25.67% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump's Tariffs Squeeze Mid-Sized Firms with Soaring Costs
data.description:
President Trump's tariffs have elevated costs for many mid-sized firms, which are defined as those with revenue between $10 million and $1 billion or employing 50-499 workers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a report from JPMorgan (JPM), monthly tariff payments by mid-sized firms have tripled since early 2025, with a notable increase beginning in April, the same month that Trump began implementing reciprocal tariffs.

International Payments Hold Steady Despite Rising Tariffs

Despite rising costs, the growth in international payments among mid-sized firms remains close to domestic payment growth. This suggests that firms are hesitant to change suppliers given existing relationships and the complexity of rerouting.

"Recent changes in U.S. trade policy have raised tariffs substantially and introduced considerable uncertainty for firms engaged in international trade," said JPMorgan.

Over the course of 2025, the average U.S. tariff rate rose to 13% from 2.6%. At the same time, the U.S. deficit barely changed, falling to $901 billion compared to $904 billion in 2024.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Pull the Trigger,' Says Investor as Micron (MU) Stock Looks Like a 'Bargain Bin' Buy
data.description:
Micron (NASDAQ:MU) shares have rallied 295% over the past year as AI-driven demand reshapes the memory market. With the industry moving into the inference phase – where models require greater capacity and bandwidth to operate at scale – Micron's memory and storage solutions are positioned at the center of that buildout.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Micron's most recent earnings report reinforced that constructive outlook. Fiscal Q1 revenue came in at $13.6 billion, up 57% year over year and 21% sequentially. Management also guided for $18.7 billion in revenue for fiscal Q2 2026, reflecting that the good times appear primed to continue.

Naturally, when a stock delivers that kind of performance, questions arise about how much upside remains. Has the bulk of the move already played out?

Investor Kennedy Njagi doesn't think so. Despite the triple-digit run, he maintains that Micron's valuation still offers attractive value relative to its growth trajectory.

"I believe that the company's valuation suggests that the market is still pricing in a downturn rather than a supercharged growth that MU is experiencing. And that pessimism creates an opportunity," explains the 5-star investor.

Njagi's thesis centers on what he sees as a structural, AI-driven memory boom. Critics who point to memory's historically cyclical nature, he contends, are missing the bigger picture. AI has fundamentally altered the equation. Memory is no longer just a supporting component tucked inside a server rack – it has become a strategic asset.

In this new paradigm, high-performance DRAM and HBM aren't interchangeable commodities. According to Njagi, they're core infrastructure for the AI economy, and Micron sits squarely at the center of that shift.

Njagi points to comments from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, who has said that modern AI data centers are increasingly constrained by memory, not compute. If that's the case, then as hyperscalers pour hundreds of billions into AI infrastructure, a meaningful slice of that capital is likely headed straight toward the companies that supply the memory backbone – with Micron at the top of that list.

When it comes to valuation, Njagi points out that MU appears vastly underpriced, even describing its Forward Non-GAAP PEG of 0.23 as being at rock bottom.

"In effect, the stock is priced as if MU's current boom will quickly fizzle out while I believe that we are just getting started with the AI driven upcycle," sums up Njagi, who rates MU shares a Strong Buy. (To watch Kennedy Njagi's track record, click here)

Wall Street is also on board the Micron express, as its 27 Buys and 2 Holds give MU a Strong Buy consensus rating. However, the rapid gains may have come a bit too quickly for some, as MU's 12-month average price target of $404.73 implies ~4% downside from current levels. Don't be surprised if those targets start moving higher in the coming weeks. (See MU stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Google Teams Up with Sea Ltd to Build AI Tools for E-Commerce, Gaming
data.description:
Alphabet-owned Google (GOOGL) has signed a new collaboration with Sea Ltd. (SE) to develop artificial intelligence (AI) tools for the Southeast Asia tech giant's e-commerce, gaming, and other businesses. The U.S.-listed shares of Sea were up 3% at the time of writing on Thursday, while GOOGL stock was flat.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Google Inks Deal with Sea Ltd for Agentic AI Tools

Under a new strategic deal, Google and Sea intend to build an agentic AI shopping prototype together on Sea's e-commerce platform, Shopee. The announcement comes as Shopee's rival Alibaba (BABA)-owned e-commerce platform Lazada unveiled a new AI model for the "agentic AI era."

Meanwhile, under its partnership with Google, Sea's gaming business, Garena, will also capitalise on AI solutions to enhance game development productivity.

The new strategic deal follows a 2024 partnership between Shoppe and Alphabet's YouTube in the Southeast Asian e-commerce market.

Overall, the partnership reflects the efforts of tech giants to monetize their AI models by offering capabilities beyond simply answering queries to handling complex workflows.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 25 Buys and seven Holds. The average GOOGL stock price target of $379.47 indicates 26.3% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Klarna Stock (KLAR) Plunges 21% as Q4 Loss Dents Banking-Led Growth
data.description:
Klarna's shares (KLAR) tumbled 22.32% after the Swedish buy-now, pay-later (BNPL) provider swung to a net loss of $26 million and reported an uneven performance in its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In the three months to December 31, Klarna posted a net loss of 19 cents per share, wider than Wall Street's expected 2-cent loss. The Stockholm-based company, which went public in New York in September, also grew its revenue by 38% year-over-year to $1.082 billion, topping analysts' estimate of $1.07 billion.

However, its net loss of $26 million marked a sharp departure from the $40 million in profit recorded in the year-ago quarter.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Will the Warner Bros. Sale Impact Theaters? The DOJ Weighs In
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) is investigating how an acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) might affect theater chains, according to Bloomberg. The agency has called major theater chains to privately discuss their views on the potential impact, including fewer theatrical releases and indirect harm to moviegoers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The scrutiny centers on Netflix's (NFLX) streaming dominance. But Hollywood also worries about Warner Bros. movies, since Netflix rarely releases movies in theaters, and only for short runs. WBD agreed to sell its studio and HBO Max to Netflix, but on Monday reopened talks with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) after a sweetened offer.

How a Merger Could Harm Theaters

Cinema United, the U.S. trade group for theater chains like AMC Entertainment (AMC) and Regal parent Cineworld Group, had earlier called a Netflix acquisition of WBD "culturally catastrophic" and a Paramount deal "no less serious." Even so, with a sale to one of them looking inevitable, theater executives feel pressured to choose sides.

In November 2025, Titanic director James Cameron backed Paramount's takeover of WBD, calling a Netflix sale "a disaster" for cinemas. Paramount CEO David Ellison told Sen. Cory Booker on Feb. 11 that Netflix's bid would "extinguish" competition, not advance it.

To counter backlash, Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos has pledged to put WBD films in cinemas exclusively for 45 days. He has also criticized Paramount's bid, arguing that the debt-heavy company will not spend as much on movies. Meanwhile, Ellison has vowed to release 30 combined films each year from Paramount and WBD if the deal proceeds.

Is WBD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Analysts remain divided on Warner Bros.' long-term prospects amid the ongoing sale drama. On TipRanks, WBD has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on four Buys and seven Hold ratings. The average Warner Bros. Discovery price target of $29.13 implies 1.2% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, WBD stock has surged over 164%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Dow Jones Slips as U.S. Trade Deficit Surges to $70 Billion
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Thursday in the red after the Commerce Department released a disappointing trade balance update.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In December, the U.S. trade deficit rose to $70.3 billion from $56.8 billion, above the consensus estimate of $55.5 billion. Imports increased by 3.6% to $357.6 billion, while exports fell by 1.7% to $287.3 billion.

U.S. Trade Deficit Remains Near Historic Highs

Rising imports weigh on gross domestic product (GDP) because they widen the trade deficit, sending more spending abroad instead of supporting domestic growth. The growth in imports was led by computer accessories, consumer goods, and copper.

For 2025, the trade deficit slightly narrowed to $901 billion from $904 billion and remains one of the highest levels since 1960. The deficit surged during the first quarter as U.S. companies rushed to front-run the Trump administration's tariffs before easing lower for the rest of the year.

data.title: S&P 500 Stumbles as Jobless Claims Drop to 5-Week Low
data.description:

For the week ended February 7, initial jobless claims fell by 23,000 to 206,000, below the consensus estimate of 225,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, increased by 17,000 to 1.869 million and were above the expectation of 1.86 million.
